mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL signals persistent inflation that erodes real earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL increases, or the rank correlation between CPIAUCSL change and 20-day returns is positive and statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, labor market deterioration triggers earnings downgrade expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are non-negative in at least 50% of instances where the trigger fires, or the mean return conditional on the trigger is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in RSAFS and decrease in UNRATE indicates robust consumer demand coinciding with labor market tightening, which amplifies corporate revenue growth expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The joint effect is stronger than either series alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and UNRATE in a linear model for 20-day forward returns does not significantly improve R-squared (F-test p > 0.05) over main effects alone, or the joint signal produces positive returns in fewer than 55% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: During restrictive monetary policy, a month-over-month decline in INDPRO signals contracting industrial capacity utilization that compresses profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3%, declining INDPRO does not precede negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of instances, or the rank IC is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects accelerating consumer spending that lifts top-line revenue expectations for S&P 500 constituents, driving positive forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS growth coincides with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rank correlation between RSAFS change and 5-day returns is negative and significant.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3%, sequential month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS accelerate discount rate pressure on equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 3%, further increases in FEDFUNDS do not produce negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of triggered months, or the mean forward return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE signals a tightening labor market that increases wage pressure and input costs, reducing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term does not add explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) versus main effects.

mechanism_narrative: HOUST falling below 1,000 thousand units signals deteriorating residential investment and weak forward economic momentum, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1,000, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months, or the mean return conditional on the trigger is not significantly below zero.
